title.zh: 卷三十五 張曹鄭列傳
title.en: Volume 35: Biographies of Zhang, Cao, Zheng
title.raw: 卷三十五 張曹鄭列傳 Volume 35: Biographies of Zhang, Cao, Zheng

sentences.zh: 張純字伯仁，京兆杜陵人也。

translations.literal: Zhang Chun, courtesy name Boren, was a native of Duling in the capital region.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun, style Boren, came from Duling in the Jingzhao commandery area.

sentences.zh: 高祖父安世，宣帝時為大司馬衛將軍，封富平侯。

translations.literal: His great-great-grandfather Anshi, in the time of Emperor Xuan, was grand marshal and general of the guard, and was enfeoffed as marquis of Fuping.
translations.idiomatic: Four generations back, his ancestor Zhang Anshi had served under Emperor Xuan as grand marshal and general of the guard and had been made marquis of Fuping.

sentences.zh: 父放，為成帝侍中。

translations.literal: His father Fang was attendant-in-chief under Emperor Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: His father Zhang Fang had been attendant-in-chief under Emperor Cheng.

sentences.zh: 純少襲爵士，哀、平間為侍中，王莽時至列卿。

translations.literal: Chun in his youth inherited the noble rank; between the reigns of Emperors Ai and Ping he was attendant-in-chief, and under Wang Mang he rose to the rank of one of the nine ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun succeeded to the title while still young; during the Ai and Ping reigns he became attendant-in-chief, and under Wang Mang he advanced to senior ministerial rank.

sentences.zh: 遭值篡偽，多亡爵士，純以敦謹守約，保全前封。

translations.literal: When usurpation occurred, many lost their noble titles, but Chun, through earnest care and faithful observance of his obligations, preserved his earlier enfeoffment.
translations.idiomatic: Many houses lost their fiefs when the usurpation came, but Zhang Chun kept his earlier grant by staying scrupulous and reliable.

sentences.zh: 建武初，先來詣闕，故得復國。

translations.literal: At the beginning of the Jianwu era he came first to the palace gates, and therefore was allowed to restore his state.
translations.idiomatic: Early in the Jianwu reign he presented himself at court ahead of others, which earned him restoration of his marquisate.

sentences.zh: 五年，拜太中大夫，使將潁川突騎安集荊、徐、楊部，督委輸，監諸將營。

translations.literal: In the fifth year he was appointed grand palace counsellor, was ordered to lead the crack cavalry of Yingchuan to pacify the Jing, Xu, and Yang regions, to supervise supply transport, and to oversee the camps of the various generals.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of Jianwu he received appointment as grand palace counsellor and was sent with Yingchuan's shock cavalry to settle the Jing, Xu, and Yang theaters, oversee convoys, and inspect the generals' encampments.

sentences.zh: 反又將兵屯田南陽，遷五官中郎將。

translations.literal: Later he also led troops in garrison farming at Nanyang, and was promoted to general of the household for all five bureaus.
translations.idiomatic: He later commanded troops on an agricultural colony at Nanyang and was raised to general of the household for all five bureaus.

sentences.zh: 有司奏，列侯非宗室不宜復國。

translations.literal: The responsible officials reported that marquises who were not members of the imperial clan ought not to have their states restored.
translations.idiomatic: The authorities memorialized that non-clan marquises should not have their kingdoms revived.

sentences.zh: 光武曰：『張純宿衛十有餘年，其勿廢，更封武始侯，食富平之半。』

translations.literal: Guangwu said: 'Zhang Chun has served in the palace guard for more than ten years; do not abolish him; instead enfeoff him as marquis of Wushi with revenue of half of Fuping.'
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu replied: "Zhang Chun has stood palace watch for over a decade; do not strip him—transfer his fief to Wushi marquisate with half the income of Fuping."

sentences.zh: 純在朝歷世，明習故事。

translations.literal: Chun had been in court for successive reigns and was thoroughly familiar with precedent.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun had served through several reigns and knew administrative precedent inside out.

sentences.zh: 建武初，舊章多闕，每有疑議，輒以訪純，自郊廟婚冠喪紀禮儀義，多所正定。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Jianwu, old regulations were largely missing; whenever there was a doubtful point, they would consult Chun; from suburban and temple rites through capping, marriage, and mourning observances, much was settled and corrected on his advice.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Jianwu the statutes were full of gaps; on every doubtful point the court turned to Zhang Chun, and he fixed much of the ritual for suburban sacrifices, temples, weddings, cappings, and mourning.

sentences.zh: 帝甚重之，以純兼虎賁中郎將，數被引見，一日或至數四。

translations.literal: The emperor greatly valued him, and appointed Chun concurrently as leader of the household gentlemen of the tiger guard; he was summoned repeatedly, sometimes four times in a single day.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor thought highly of him, gave him the concurrent post of leader of the household gentlemen of the tiger guard, and called him in so often that he might be seen four times in one day.

sentences.zh: 純以宗廟未定，昭穆失序，十九年，乃與太仆朱浮共奏言：『陛下興於匹庶，蕩滌天下，誅鋤暴亂，興繼祖宗。

translations.literal: Chun, because the ancestral temples were not yet fixed and the zhao-mu order was confused, in the nineteenth year together with the grand coachman Zhu Fu jointly submitted a memorial saying: 'Your Majesty rose from commoner status, swept away the realm, punished violence and disorder, and restored the line of the ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: In the nineteenth year of Jianwu, because the imperial shrines were unsettled and the zhao-mu sequence wrong, he and grand coachman Zhu Fu jointly argued: "You rose from outside the court, purged the empire, crushed rebellion, and restored the ancestral house.

sentences.zh: 竊以經義所紀，人事眾心，雖實同創革，而名為中興，宜奉先帝，恭承祭祀者也。

translations.literal: According to what the classics record and what human affairs and popular sentiment require, although in substance it is the same as founding anew, in name it is a restoration; one ought to honor the former emperors and respectfully undertake the sacrifices.'
translations.idiomatic: The canon and common expectation alike treat this as a restoration in name though like a new foundation; the former emperors must be honored and their cult maintained."

sentences.zh: 元帝以來，宗廟奉祠高皇帝為受命祖，孝文皇帝為太宗，孝武皇帝為世宗，皆如舊制。

translations.literal: From Emperor Yuan onward, in the ancestral temple the high emperor was honored as the ancestor who received the mandate, Emperor Xiaowen as the great ancestor, and Emperor Wu as the exalted ancestor, all according to the old system.
translations.idiomatic: Since Emperor Yuan, the high temple had worshipped Gaozu as the recipient of the mandate, Emperor Wen as the great progenitor, and Emperor Wu as the exalted ancestor, following the old pattern.

sentences.zh: 又立親廟四世，推南頓君以上盡於舂陵節侯。

translations.literal: In addition four generations of personal temples were established, tracing from Lord Nandun upward to Marquis Jieling of Chunling.
translations.idiomatic: Four generations of "close" shrines had also been set, running from Lord Nandun back to Marquis Jieling of Chunling.

sentences.zh: 禮，為人後者則為之子，既事大宗，則降其私親。

translations.literal: By ritual, one who becomes heir to another line becomes that line's son; once the great descent is served, one's private kin is demoted in rank.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual says an adopted heir is a son of the line he enters; serving the great trunk means demoting one's own blood relatives.

sentences.zh: 今禘祫高廟，陳序昭穆，而舂陵四世，君臣並列，以卑廁尊，不合禮意，設不遭王莽，而國嗣無寄，推求宗室，以陛下繼統者，安得復顧私親，違禮制乎？

translations.literal: Now at the di and xia sacrifices in the high temple the zhao-mu order is arrayed, yet the four Chunling generations stand lord and minister side by side, placing the low beside the high, which does not accord with ritual meaning; supposing Wang Mang had not appeared and the imperial succession had nowhere to turn, and one sought among the imperial house for one to continue the line whom Your Majesty succeeded—how could one again heed private kin and violate the ritual system?
translations.idiomatic: At the great di and xia rites in the high temple the generations are ordered, yet the four Chunling generations appear as lord and subject together—base beside exalted—which breaks ritual. Had Wang Mang never seized power and the throne lacked an heir, and a clansman had been chosen for you to succeed, would private kin still override the code?

sentences.zh: 昔高帝以自受命，不由太上，宣帝以孫後祖，不敢私親，故為父立廟，獨群臣侍祠。

translations.literal: Formerly the high emperor, because he received the mandate himself, did not derive it from the supreme emperor; Emperor Xuan, as a grandson who honored an ancestor, did not dare favor private kin, and therefore established a temple for his father with only the ministers attending the sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu took the mandate in his own person, not from a "supreme emperor"; Emperor Xuan, exalting an ancestor as a grandson, still shrank from privileging kin and built a father-temple served only by ministers.

sentences.zh: 臣愚謂宜除今親廟，以則二帝舊典，願下有司博采其議。

translations.literal: Your servant foolishly thinks the present personal temples ought to be abolished to follow the precedent of the two emperors; I beg that the matter be referred to the officials for wide discussion.'
translations.idiomatic: I would abolish the current "close" shrines to match those two precedents and ask that the ministries debate this broadly."

sentences.zh: 』詔下公卿，大司徒戴涉、大司空竇融議：『宜以宣、元、成、哀、平五帝四世代今親廟，宣、元皇帝尊為祖、父，可親奉祠，成帝以下，有司行事，別為南頓君立皇考廟。

translations.literal: An edict was issued to the dukes and ministers; grand minister over the masses Dai She and grand minister of works Dou Rong deliberated: 'The five emperors Yuan, Cheng, Ai, and Ping with four generations should replace the present personal temples; Emperors Xuan and Yuan should be honored as grandfather and father and may receive sacrifice in person; from Emperor Cheng downward the officials should perform the rites; separately establish a temple to the imperial father for Lord Nandun.
translations.idiomatic: The edict went to the high ministers. Dai She and Dou Rong proposed: "Use the five rulers from Xuan through Ping, with four generations, in place of today's private shrines; Xuan and Yuan as grandfather and father may receive worship from you in person; from Cheng on let officials officiate; add a separate "imperial father" temple for Lord Nandun.

sentences.zh: 其祭上至舂陵節侯，群臣奉祠，以明尊尊之敬，親親之恩。

translations.literal: Let sacrifice extend upward to Marquis Jieling of Chunling, with the ministers performing the cult, to clarify the respect due to exalted rank and the kindness due to close kin.'
translations.idiomatic: Worship should reach up to Marquis Jieling of Chunling with ministers conducting it, to mark both reverence for rank and affection for kin."

sentences.zh: 』帝從之。

translations.literal: The emperor followed this.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor accepted their recommendation.

sentences.zh: 是時宗廟未備，自元帝以上，祭於洛陽高廟，成帝以下，祠於長安高廟，其南頓四世，隨所在而祭焉。

translations.literal: At that time the ancestral shrines were not complete; from Emperor Yuan upward sacrifice was at the high temple in Luoyang; from Emperor Cheng downward it was at the high temple in Chang'an; the four Nandun generations were sacrificed to wherever they were located.
translations.idiomatic: The national shrines were still incomplete: from Yuan upward rites were at Luoyang's high temple, from Cheng downward at Chang'an's high temple, while the four Nandun generations were honored wherever their tablets happened to be.

sentences.zh: 明年，純代朱浮為太仆。

translations.literal: The next year Chun replaced Zhu Fu as grand coachman.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Zhang Chun succeeded Zhu Fu as grand coachman.

sentences.zh: 二十三年，代杜林為大司空。

translations.literal: In the twenty-third year he replaced Du Lin as grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 23 he replaced Du Lin as grand minister of works.

sentences.zh: 在位慕曹參之跡，務於無為，選辟椽史，皆知名大儒。

translations.literal: In office he emulated Cao Shen's course and devoted himself to non-action; in selecting clerks and secretaries he took only noted great scholars.
translations.idiomatic: In office he modeled himself on Cao Shen's quiet governance and filled his bureau with eminent Confucians.

sentences.zh: 明年，上穿陽渠，引洛水為漕，百姓得其利。

translations.literal: The next year the emperor opened the Yang channel, brought the Luo River into the transport canal, and the common people benefited.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the court cut the Yang canal to feed the Luo into the grain waterways, to the people's profit.

sentences.zh: 二十六年，詔純曰：『禘、祫之祭，不行已久矣。

translations.literal: In the twenty-sixth year an edict to Chun said: 'The di and xia sacrifices have not been carried out for a long time.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 26 an edict told him: "The di and xia sacrifices have long been neglected.

sentences.zh: 「三年不為禮，禮必壞；

translations.literal: "If for three years one does not practice ritual, ritual is sure to decay;
translations.idiomatic: "If ritual is neglected for three years, it will decay;

sentences.zh: 三年不為樂，樂必崩」。

translations.literal: if for three years one does not practice music, music is sure to collapse."
translations.idiomatic: if music is neglected for three years, it will fall apart."

sentences.zh: 宜據經典，詳為其制。

translations.literal: You should rely on the classics and set out the system in detail.'
translations.idiomatic: Ground the schedule in the classics and spell it out in detail."

sentences.zh: 』純奏曰：『《禮》，三年一祫，五年一禘。

translations.literal: Chun submitted a memorial saying: 'According to the Rites, once every three years there is a xia, once every five years a di.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun replied: "The Rites prescribe a xia every three years and a di every five.

sentences.zh: 《春秋傳》曰：「大祫者何？

translations.literal: The Zuo commentary asks: "What is the great xia?
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo asks what the great xia is:

sentences.zh: 合祭也。

translations.literal: It is a joint sacrifice.'
translations.idiomatic: a combined offering to all ancestors."

sentences.zh: 」毀廟及未毀廟之主皆登，合食乎太祖，五年而再殷。

translations.literal: The destroyed-temple and not-yet-destroyed-temple lords all ascend and share food with the grand ancestor; once every five years there is a second great assembly.
translations.idiomatic: Tablets from demolished and standing shrines alike ascend to feast with the first ancestor; the grand assembly recurs every five years.

sentences.zh: 漢舊制三年一祫，毀廟主合良高廟，存廟主未嘗合祭。

translations.literal: Han's old system was a xia every three years: destroyed-temple lords were brought together at Gaozu's temple, while lords of standing temples had never been jointly sacrificed.
translations.idiomatic: Under Han practice a triennial xia joined the deposed-line tablets at Gaozu's temple, whereas lines still in their own shrines had not been merged in one rite.

sentences.zh: 元始五年，諸王公列侯廟會，始為禘祭。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of the Yuanshi reign, when the kings, dukes, marquises, and temples assembled, the di sacrifice was first instituted.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshi 5, when the nobles and shrines assembled, the di rite was first performed.

sentences.zh: 又前十八年親幸長安，亦行此禮。

translations.literal: Moreover eighteen years before Your Majesty personally visited Chang'an and also performed this rite.
translations.idiomatic: Eighteen years earlier you had gone to Chang'an in person and observed the same ceremony.

sentences.zh: 禮說三年一閏，天氣小備；

translations.literal: Ritual theory says that in three years there is one intercalary month, when minor completeness of heaven's qi is achieved;
translations.idiomatic: Canonists compare the three-year cycle to one intercalation—heaven's breath partly restored;

sentences.zh: 五年再閏，天氣大備。

translations.literal: in five years there are two intercalary months, when great completeness of heaven's qi is achieved.
translations.idiomatic: the five-year span to two intercalations—full restoration of the seasonal order.

sentences.zh: 故三年一祫，五年一禘。

translations.literal: Therefore once every three years there is a xia, once every five years a di.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the triennial xia and the quinquennial di.

sentences.zh: 禘之為言諦，諦定昭穆尊卑之義也。

translations.literal: The word di means "examine"; it settles the meaning of zhao-mu and exalted versus humble.
translations.idiomatic: Di means "to sort": it fixes who is zhao, who is mu, who ranks above whom.

sentences.zh: 禘祭以夏四月，夏者陽氣在上，陰氣在下，故正尊卑之義也。

translations.literal: The di is held in the fourth month of summer, when yang qi is above and yin qi below, and therefore rectifies the meaning of exalted and humble.
translations.idiomatic: The di falls in summer's fourth month, yang aloft and yin below, the right season to order high and low.

sentences.zh: 祫祭以冬十月，冬者五谷成孰，物備禮成，故合聚飲食也。

translations.literal: The xia is held in the tenth month of winter, when the five grains are ripe, things are ready and the rites complete, and therefore gathers all for a communal feast.
translations.idiomatic: The xia comes in winter's tenth month, when grain is in, goods gathered, and the rites complete—time for the great common meal.

sentences.zh: 斯典之廢，於茲八年，謂可如禮施行，以時定議。

translations.literal: This canon has been in abeyance for eight years now; I say it may be practiced according to ritual and a decision fixed in due season.'
translations.idiomatic: That norm has lapsed eight years; it should be restored on schedule and the calendar fixed."

sentences.zh: 』定從之，自是禘、祫遂定。

translations.literal: The decision followed this, and thereafter di and xia were fixed.
translations.idiomatic: The court agreed, and from then on di and xia were on the books.

sentences.zh: 時，南單於及烏桓來降，邊境無事，百姓新去兵革，歲仍有年，家給人足。

translations.literal: At that time the southern Shanyu and the Wuhuan had submitted; the border was quiet; the people had newly been delivered from arms; successive years brought harvests; every household had enough.
translations.idiomatic: The southern Shanyu and Wuhuan had surrendered, the frontier was calm, the realm had just put down weapons, harvests ran in succession, and families were well fed.

sentences.zh: 純以聖王之建辟雍，所以崇尊禮義，既富而教者也。

translations.literal: Chun held that when the sage kings established the Bright Hall and ringed ritual hall, it was to exalt ritual and righteousness and to teach those already enriched.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun argued that the sage-kings built the Bright Hall and the ritual enclosure to magnify li and yi and to instruct a people already prosperous.

sentences.zh: 乃案七經讖、明堂圖、河間《古辟雍記》、孝武太山明堂制度，及平帝時議，欲具奏之。

translations.literal: He therefore collated the prognostications on the seven classics, the Bright Hall chart, the ancient Bright Hall record from Hejian, Emperor Wu's Bright Hall regulations on Mount Tai, and the discussions from Emperor Ping's time, intending to submit them all in a memorial.
translations.idiomatic: He collated the seven-classics weft-texts, a Bright Hall diagram, the Hejian "Ancient Bright Hall" record, Emperor Wu's Tai-shan Bright Hall plan, and Ping-era debates, preparing a full memorial.

sentences.zh: 未及上，會博士桓榮上言宜立辟雍、明堂，章下三公、太常，而純議同榮，帝乃許之。

translations.literal: Before he could submit it, the erudite Huan Rong submitted a statement that the Bright Hall and Bright Hall ought to be established; the document was sent down to the three dukes and the grand master of ceremonies, and since Chun's opinion agreed with Rong's, the emperor approved.
translations.idiomatic: Before he could present it, Erudite Huan Rong urged building Bright Hall and the ritual hall; the memorial went to the three dukes and the grand master of ceremonies, Zhang Chun concurred with Huan Rong, and the emperor approved.

sentences.zh: 三十年，純奏上宜封禪，曰：『自古受命而帝，治世之隆，必有封禪，以告成功焉。

translations.literal: In the thirtieth year Chun submitted that feng and shan ought to be performed, saying: 'From antiquity, one who received the mandate and became emperor, at the height of a well-ordered age, invariably performed feng and shan to announce success.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 30 he urged feng and shan: "Since antiquity every true recipient of the mandate, at the zenith of good rule, has mounted Tai to announce success.

sentences.zh: 《樂動聲儀》曰：「以《雅》治人，《風》成於《頌》。

translations.literal: The Yue dong sheng yi says: "With the Ya one governs men; with the Feng the Sheng is completed in the Song.'"
translations.idiomatic: The Yue dong sheng yi says: The Ya orders the people; the Feng and Song crown the ode."

sentences.zh: 」有周之盛，成、康之間，郊配封禪，皆可見也。

translations.literal: At the height of Zhou, between Kings Cheng and Kang, suburban pairing and feng and shan are all visible.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou's glory under Cheng and Kang shows suburban pairing and feng-shan alike.

sentences.zh: 書曰：「歲二月，東巡狩，至於岱宗，祡」，則封禪之義也。

translations.literal: The Documents say: "In the second month of the year, he toured east to the hunting inspection, arriving at Daizong and performing the chai offering"—this is the meaning of feng and shan.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents record the second-month eastern inspection at Daizong with the chai offering—that is the sense of feng and shan.

sentences.zh: 臣伏見陛下受中興之命，平海內之亂，修復祖宗，撫存萬姓，天下曠然，咸蒙更生，恩德雲行，惠澤雨施，黎元安寧，夷狄慕義。

translations.literal: Your servant has observed that Your Majesty received the mandate of restoration, pacified rebellion within the seas, repaired the work of the ancestors, and cherished the myriad households; the realm is open and clear, all have received new life; favor moves like clouds, kindness falls like rain; the black-haired people are at peace and the barbarians admire righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: I have watched you take up the restoration mandate, end civil war, repair the ancestral enterprise, and nurture the people: the land lies open, all feel reborn, grace spreads like clouds, bounty falls like rain, commoners rest easy, and foreigners incline to duty.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：『受天之祜，四方來賀。

translations.literal: The Odes say: 'Receiving heaven's blessing, the four quarters come with congratulations.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes say: Heaven's blessing draws tribute from every quarter.

sentences.zh: 」今攝提之歲，倉龍甲寅，德在東宮。

translations.literal: Now in the year-star of Sheti, the azure dragon jia-yin year, virtue lies in the eastern palace.
translations.idiomatic: This is the Sheti year, jia-yin of the azure dragon, with virtue in the eastern quarter.

sentences.zh: 宜及嘉時，遵唐帝之典，繼孝武之業，以二月東巡狩，封於岱宗，明中興，勒功勛，復祖統，報天神，禪梁父，祀地祇，傳祚子孫，萬世之基也。

translations.literal: It is fitting to seize this auspicious season, follow the canon of Emperor Tang, continue the enterprise of Emperor Wu, in the second month tour east for the hunting inspection, perform feng at Daizong to manifest the restoration, inscribe merit, restore the ancestral alignment, repay heaven above, perform shan at Liangfu, sacrifice to earth below, and transmit the foundation to posterity for ten thousand generations.'
translations.idiomatic: You should use this propitious season, follow Yao's model and Emperor Wu's example, tour east in the second month, feng Mount Tai to proclaim the restoration, record achievements, realign the cult with the founders, thank heaven above, shan at Liangfu, honor earth below, and lay a foundation for endless generations."

sentences.zh: 』中元元年，帝乃東巡岱宗，以純視禦史大夫從，並上元封舊儀及刻石文。

translations.literal: In the first year of the Zhongyuan era the emperor then toured east to Daizong, with Chun acting as imperial counsellor in attendance, and together presented the old Yuanfeng protocol and the inscribed stone texts.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongyuan 1 the emperor traveled east to Tai; Zhang Chun attended as acting imperial counsellor and brought up the Yuanfeng precedents and the stele texts.

sentences.zh: 三月，薨，謚曰節候。

translations.literal: In the third month he died; his posthumous title was marquis Jie.
translations.idiomatic: He died in the third month and was posthumously titled marquis Jie.

sentences.zh: 子 奮

translations.literal: Son: Fen.
translations.idiomatic: His son was Zhang Fen.

sentences.zh: 奮字稚通。

translations.literal: Fen, courtesy name Zhitong.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fen, style Zhitong.

sentences.zh: 父純，臨終敕家丞曰：『司空無功於時，猥蒙爵士，身死之後，勿議傳國。

translations.literal: His father Chun, at the end of his life, instructed the household steward: 'The grand minister of works has no merit for the age yet has undeservedly received rank and title; after my death do not discuss passing on the state.'
translations.idiomatic: On his deathbed Zhang Chun told his household steward: "I have done nothing to earn the age's praise yet hold rank and fief; after I die do not press for transmission of the title."

sentences.zh: 』奮兄根，少被病，光武詔奮嗣爵，奮稱純遺敕，固不肯受。

translations.literal: Fen's elder brother Gen from youth suffered from illness; Guangwu ordered Fen to inherit the title, but Fen cited Chun's dying instruction and firmly refused to accept.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fen's elder brother Gen had been ill since youth; Guangwu ordered Fen to inherit, but Fen cited his father's last words and refused.

sentences.zh: 帝以奮違詔，敕收下獄，奮惶怖，乃襲封。

translations.literal: The emperor, because Fen disobeyed the edict, ordered him seized and imprisoned; Fen was frightened and then accepted the enfeoffment.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor took this as defiance of an edict and had him jailed; terrified, Zhang Fen then took the title.

sentences.zh: 永平四年，隨例歸國。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yongping he returned to his state according to the usual rule.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 4 he surrendered his fief under the general rule.

sentences.zh: 時歲災旱，祈雨不應，乃上表曰：『比年不登，人用饑匱，今復久旱，秋稼未立，陽氣垂盡，歲月迫促。

translations.literal: At that time successive years brought drought and disaster; prayers for rain brought no response; he therefore submitted a memorial saying: 'In recent years harvests have failed and the people lack grain; now there has been prolonged drought and the autumn crop is not yet planted; yang qi is nearly exhausted and the year presses on.
translations.idiomatic: Years of drought had followed one another; rain rituals failed. He memorialized: "Harvests have failed and the people are hungry; the drought drags on and autumn grain is not yet in the ground; yang is almost spent and the season runs out.

sentences.zh: 夫國以民為本，民以谷為命，政之急務，憂之重者也。

translations.literal: The state takes the people as its root, the people take grain as their life; this is the most urgent task of government and the weightiest concern.
translations.idiomatic: The state rests on the people, the people on grain—nothing in government is more urgent or more worrisome.

sentences.zh: 臣蒙恩尤深，受職過任，夙夜憂懼，章奏不能敘心，願對中常侍疏奏。

translations.literal: Your servant has received favor especially deep and holds office beyond his capacity; day and night he worries, and memorials cannot fully express his mind; he begs to state his case orally before the regular palace attendants.'
translations.idiomatic: I am more deeply favored than I deserve and hold a post above my strength; I fret day and night and paper cannot say it all—I ask leave to speak face to face before the regular palace attendants."

sentences.zh: 』即時引見，復口陳時政之宜。

translations.literal: He was immediately granted an audience and again orally set forth what was fitting for the times.
translations.idiomatic: He was summoned at once and repeated his policy advice in person.

sentences.zh: 明日，和帝召太尉、司徒幸洛陽獄，錄囚徒，收洛陽令陳歆，即大雨三日。

translations.literal: The next day Emperor He summoned the grand commandant and minister of education to the Luoyang prison to review the convicts, arrested the magistrate of Luoyang Chen Xin, and at once there was rain for three days.
translations.idiomatic: The next day Emperor He sent the grand commandant and minister of education to review prisoners at the Luoyang jail, arrested magistrate Chen Xin, and rain fell for three days.

sentences.zh: 奮在位清白，無他異績。

translations.literal: Fen while in office was incorrupt and had no other remarkable achievements.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fen's tenure was clean but otherwise unremarkable.

sentences.zh: 九年，以病罷。

translations.literal: In the ninth year he resigned because of illness.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year he stepped down on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 在家上疏曰：『聖人所美，政道至要，本在禮樂。

translations.literal: At home he submitted a memorial saying: 'What the sages praise and what is most essential in governing the way fundamentally lies in ritual and music.
translations.idiomatic: From retirement he wrote: "The sages prized above all the essentials of good rule—ritual and music.

sentences.zh: 《五經》同歸，而禮樂之用尤急。

translations.literal: The Five Classics return to one purpose, yet the use of ritual and music is especially pressing.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Classics converge on one aim, yet ritual and music matter most urgently.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「安上治民，莫善於禮；

translations.literal: Confucius said: "To secure superiors and govern the people, nothing is better than ritual;
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, Nothing steadies ruler and people like ritual;

sentences.zh: 移風易俗，莫善於樂。

translations.literal: to change customs and transform the people, nothing is better than music.'
translations.idiomatic: nothing changes manners like music.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「揖讓而化天下者，禮樂之謂也。

translations.literal: He also said: "He who with yielding courtesy transforms the world speaks of ritual and music.'
translations.idiomatic: He also said, He who remolds the world through deference does it with ritual and music.

sentences.zh: 」先王之道，禮樂可謂盛矣。

translations.literal: The way of the former kings—ritual and music may be called splendid.
translations.idiomatic: The way of the ancient kings shows how splendid ritual and music can be.

sentences.zh: 孔子謂子夏曰：「禮以修外，樂以制內，丘已矣夫！

translations.literal: Confucius said to Zi Xia: "Ritual adorns the outer, music regulates the inner—I am finished!'
translations.idiomatic: To Zi Xia he said, Ritual dresses the outside, music orders the inside—there I rest.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「禮樂不興，則刑罰不中；

translations.literal: He also said: "If ritual and music do not flourish, punishments will not hit the mark;
translations.idiomatic: He added, When ritual and music fail, penalties go awry;

sentences.zh: 刑罰不中，則民無所厝其手足。

translations.literal: if punishments do not hit the mark, the people have nowhere to put hand or foot.'
translations.idiomatic: when penalties go awry, the people do not know where to set hand or foot.

sentences.zh: 」臣以為漢當制作禮樂，是以先帝聖德，數下詔書，湣傷崩缺，而眾儒不達，議多駁異。

translations.literal: Your servant thinks that Han ought to produce ritual and music; for this reason former emperors with sagely virtue repeatedly issued edicts, grieving at gaps and losses, yet the many scholars did not understand and their opinions were mostly contradictory.
translations.idiomatic: I believe Han should promulgate ritual and music; past sage emperors sent edict after edict lamenting the gaps, but the scholars quarreled and contradicted one another.

sentences.zh: 臣累世臺輔，而大典未定，私竊惟憂，不忘寢食。

translations.literal: Your servant's family for generations has stood at the highest councils, yet the great canons are not fixed; privately he worries and cannot forget it waking or sleeping.
translations.idiomatic: My house has served at the summit for generations, yet the great codes remain unsettled—I cannot sleep or eat for thinking of it.

sentences.zh: 臣犬馬齒盡，誠冀先死見禮樂之定。

translations.literal: Your servant's dog-and-horse teeth are at an end; he sincerely hopes before death to see ritual and music fixed.'
translations.idiomatic: I am old as a spent horse and only hope to see ritual and music settled before I die."

sentences.zh: 』十三年，更召拜太常。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year he was again summoned and appointed grand master of ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year he was recalled and made grand master of ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 復上疏曰：『漢當改作禮樂，圖書著明。

translations.literal: He again submitted a memorial saying: 'Han ought to reform and produce ritual and music; the charts and documents make this clear.
translations.idiomatic: He wrote again: "Han must revise and institute ritual and music—the charts and texts say so plainly.

sentences.zh: 王者化定制禮，功成作樂。

translations.literal: When a king's transformation is fixed he orders ritual, when his merit is complete he makes music.
translations.idiomatic: When a king's civilizing work is set he prescribes ritual; when his achievement is full he composes music.

sentences.zh: 謹條禮樂異議三事，願下有司，以時考定。

translations.literal: Respectfully I list three points of dispute on ritual and music; I beg that they be sent to the officials for timely determination.
translations.idiomatic: I list three disputed points on ritual and music and ask the ministries to settle them soon.

sentences.zh: 昔者孝武皇帝、光武皇帝封禪告成，而禮樂不定，事不相副。

translations.literal: Formerly Emperor Xiaowu and Emperor Guangwu performed feng and shan to announce completion, yet ritual and music were not fixed—the deeds did not match the words.
translations.idiomatic: Emperors Wu and Guangwu had already reported success on Tai, yet ritual and music stayed unsettled—deed and word did not match.

sentences.zh: 先帝已詔曹褒，今陛下但奉而成之，猶周公斟酌文武之道，非自為制，誠無所疑。

translations.literal: The late emperor already ordered Cao Bao; now Your Majesty need only carry it out, like the Duke of Zhou's weighing of the ways of Wen and Wu—not making law on one's own—and truly there is nothing to doubt.
translations.idiomatic: The late emperor commissioned Cao Bao; you need only enact his work, as the Duke of Zhou adjusted Wen and Wu without inventing law—there is no real doubt here.

sentences.zh: 久執謙謙，令大漢之業不以時成，非所以章顯祖宗功德，建太平之基，為後世法。

translations.literal: To hold long to excessive modesty and not let the great enterprise of Han be completed in its season is not the way to display the merit of the ancestors, establish the foundation of great peace, or be a model for later ages.'
translations.idiomatic: Endless modesty that delays Han's great work ill serves the ancestors' glory, the peace of the realm, or an example to posterity."

sentences.zh: 』帝雖善之，猶未施行。

translations.literal: The emperor though he approved it still did not put it into practice.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor praised the plea but did not act on it.

sentences.zh: 其冬，復以病罷。

translations.literal: That winter he again resigned because of illness.
translations.idiomatic: That winter he again retired ill.

sentences.zh: 明年，卒於家。

translations.literal: The next year he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he died at home.

sentences.zh: 子甫嗣，官至津城門候。

translations.literal: His son Fu succeeded him; he rose in office to captain of the Jin-cheng gate.
translations.idiomatic: His son Zhang Fu inherited the title and rose to captain of the Jin-cheng gate.

sentences.zh: 甫卒，子吉嗣。

translations.literal: When Fu died, his son Ji succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Fu died, his son Ji succeeded.

sentences.zh: 永初三年，吉卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yongchu Ji died without a son and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 3 Ji died sonless and the marquisate was struck off.

sentences.zh: 自昭帝封安世，至吉，傳國八世，經歷篡亂，二百年間未嘗譴黜，封者莫與為此。

translations.literal: From Emperor Zhao's enfeoffment of Anshi down to Ji, the state was transmitted through eight generations; through two hundred years of usurpation and turmoil it was never once degraded or removed—no other enfeoffed house matched this.
translations.idiomatic: From Zhang Anshi's fief under Emperor Zhao to Zhang Ji, the line held the marquisate through eight generations and two centuries of chaos without a single demotion—no other house matched that record.

sentences.zh: 褒少篤誌，有大度，結發傳充業，博雅疏通，尤好禮事。

translations.literal: Bao from youth was steadfast in purpose, had breadth of mind, inherited the family enterprise upon capping, was erudite, broad, and perspicacious, and especially loved matters of ritual.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Bao was serious-minded from boyhood, generous in spirit, took up the family calling at adulthood, was learned and clear-thinking, and cared above all for ritual.

sentences.zh: 常感朝廷制度未備，慕叔孫通為漢禮儀，晝夜研精，沈吟專思，寢則懷抱筆劄，行則誦習文書，當其念至，忘所之適。

translations.literal: He constantly felt that court institutions were incomplete and admired Shusun Tong for having made Han ritual; day and night he studied intently, pondering in concentration; in bed he kept brush and slips in his arms, on the road he recited documents, and when a thought came he forgot where he was going.
translations.idiomatic: He felt the dynasty's ceremonial code was still half-built and modeled himself on Shusun Tong; he pored over texts day and night, slept with brush and tablets in his belt, muttered classics on the road, and when an idea struck he would lose track of his path.

sentences.zh: 初舉孝廉，再遷圉令，以禮理人，以德化俗。

translations.literal: At first he was recommended as filial and incorrupt, was twice promoted to magistrate of Yu, governed the people by ritual, and transformed custom by virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filial and honest, he rose twice to magistrate of Yu, ruling through ritual and reshaping manners with virtue.

sentences.zh: 時它郡盜徒五人來入圉界，吏捕得入，陳留太守馬嚴聞而疾惡，風縣殺之。

translations.literal: At that time five bandits from another commandery entered Yu's borders; the clerks seized and brought them in; the grand administrator of Chenliu Ma Yan heard of it and detested them, and hinted to the county to kill them.
translations.idiomatic: Five thieves from a neighboring district crossed into Yu; his officers arrested them; Ma Yan, prefect of Chenliu, heard the news, hated the criminals, and signaled the county to execute them.

sentences.zh: 褒敕吏曰：『夫絕人命者，天亦絕之。

translations.literal: Bao instructed the clerks: 'He who cuts short human life, heaven also cuts short his.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Bao told his staff: "Those who take life will have life taken from them.

sentences.zh: 臯陶不為盜制死刑，管仲遇盜而升諸公。

translations.literal: Gao Yao did not prescribe death for robbers, and when Guan Zhong met robbers he advanced them to the duke.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao did not fix death as the penalty for theft; Guan Zhong once promoted thieves to the lord's notice.

sentences.zh: 今承旨而殺之，是逆天心，順府意也，其罰重矣。

translations.literal: Now to kill them on orders from above is to oppose heaven's mind and comply with the prefect's wish—the penalty would be heavy.
translations.idiomatic: To kill them now on a superior's nod would defy heaven and merely please the prefect—that would be the graver fault.

sentences.zh: 如得全此人命而身坐之，吾所願也。

translations.literal: If I can preserve this man's life while myself bearing the punishment, that is what I wish.'
translations.idiomatic: If I may save their lives and take the blame myself, I will gladly do it."

sentences.zh: 』遂不為殺。

translations.literal: Therefore he did not kill them.
translations.idiomatic: He refused to execute them.

sentences.zh: 嚴奏褒耎弱，免官歸郡，為功曹。

translations.literal: Yan memorialized that Bao was weak and irresolute; he was dismissed and returned to his commandery, where he became chief clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yan denounced him as soft; Cao Bao was stripped of office and went home to serve as merit clerk.

sentences.zh: 征拜博士。

translations.literal: He was summoned and appointed erudite.
translations.idiomatic: The court summoned him as an erudite.

sentences.zh: 會肅宗欲制定禮樂，元和二年下詔曰：『《河圖》稱「赤九會昌，十世以光，十一以興」。

translations.literal: When Emperor Zhang wished to fix ritual and music, in the second year of the Yuanhe era he issued an edict saying: 'The Hetu says "The red nine will meet prosperity; the tenth generation will shine; the eleventh will rise."
translations.idiomatic: As Emperor Zhang (Suzong) planned to codify ritual and music, in Yuanhe 2 he proclaimed: "The River Chart says: The red nine shall flourish; the tenth line shall blaze; the eleventh shall rise.

sentences.zh: 《尚書璇機鈐》曰：「述堯理世，平制禮樂，放唐之文。

translations.literal: The Shang shu xuan ji qian says: "He continued Yao's ordering of the age, balanced ritual and music, and released the pattern of Tang."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu xuan ji qian says: He followed Yao in ordering the world, set ritual and music in balance, and unfolded Tang's pattern.

sentences.zh: 」予末小子，托於數終，曷以纘興，崇弘祖宗，仁濟元元？

translations.literal: I, the least of sons, rely on the end of the reckoning—how shall I continue the rise, exalt the ancestors, and with kindness succor the common people?
translations.idiomatic: I, a mere child, rest on the close of the cycle—how am I to carry on the revival, magnify the forebears, and bring mercy to the people?

sentences.zh: 《帝命驗》曰：「順堯考德，題期立象。

translations.literal: The Di ming yan says: "He followed Yao and examined virtue, fixed the term, and established the symbols."
translations.idiomatic: The Di ming yan says: He followed Yao, tested virtue, set the calendar, and raised the emblems.

sentences.zh: 」且三五步驟，優劣殊軌，況予頑陋，無以克堪，雖欲從之，末由也已。

translations.literal: Moreover the paces of the Three and Five differ in excellence and inferiority follow different tracks—how much more I, stubborn and dull, unable to bear the burden; though I wish to follow them, I have no way to do so.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Ages and Five Rulers each trod a different path of better and worse—far more then may I, coarse and limited, bear the load; I would follow them yet find no road.

sentences.zh: 每見圖書，中心恧焉。

translations.literal: Whenever I see the charts and documents, my heart is ashamed.'
translations.idiomatic: Whenever I read those prophecies, I am abashed."

sentences.zh: 』褒知帝旨欲有興作，乃上疏曰：『昔者聖人受命而王，莫不制禮作樂，以著功德。

translations.literal: Bao knew the emperor's purpose was to undertake something; he therefore submitted a memorial saying: 'Formerly when sages received the mandate and became kings, none failed to fix ritual and make music, thereby displaying merit.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Bao saw the sovereign meant to build something lasting, and wrote: "Every sage who took the mandate and rose as king fashioned ritual and music to display achievement.

sentences.zh: 功成作樂，化定制禮，所以救世俗，致禎祥，為萬姓獲福於皇天者也。

translations.literal: When merit was complete they made music; when transformation was fixed they ordered ritual—thereby to rescue the vulgar age, bring auspicious omens, and win blessing for the myriad surnames from august heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Work finished, they composed music; custom settled, they prescribed ritual—thus to redeem a coarse age, draw down good omens, and win heaven's blessing for the people.

sentences.zh: 今皇天降祉，嘉瑞並臻，制作之符，甚於言語。

translations.literal: Now august heaven sends blessing, auspicious signs arrive together, and the tokens for making institutions speak louder than words.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven now showers favor, prodigies crowd in, and the signs for legislation outshout mere speech.

sentences.zh: 宜定文制，著成漢禮，丕顯祖宗盛德之美。

translations.literal: It is fitting to fix the pattern of writing, complete Han ritual, and grandly display the splendor of the ancestors' abundant virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: It is time to set the literary code, finish the Han ritual canon, and make plain the ancestors' shining virtue."

sentences.zh: 』章下太常，太常巢堪以為一世大典，非褒所定，不可許。

translations.literal: The memorial was sent down to the grand master of ceremonies; the grand master of ceremonies Chao Kan held that a great canon for an entire age could not be fixed by Bao and ought not to be approved.
translations.idiomatic: The edict went to the grand master of ceremonies Chao Kan, who replied that a code for an entire reign could not be settled by Cao Bao and should be denied.

sentences.zh: 帝知群僚拘攣，難與圖始，朝廷禮憲，宜時刊立，明年復下詔曰：『朕以不德，膺祖宗弘烈。

translations.literal: The emperor knew the host of officials were cramped and hard to start on a new plan, yet court ritual and statutes ought to be published in due course; the next year he again issued an edict saying: 'I, lacking virtue, have received the great enterprise of the ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor knew his ministers were timid starters, but national ritual needed writing down; the next year he said again: "I lack virtue yet shoulder my forebears' vast charge.

sentences.zh: 乃者鸞鳳仍集，麟龍並臻，甘露宵降，嘉谷滋生，赤草之類，紀於史官。

translations.literal: Of late phoenixes have repeatedly gathered, unicorn and dragon have both arrived, sweet dew has fallen by night, fine grain has sprouted, and things like red grass have been recorded by the historiographers.
translations.idiomatic: Lately phoenixes flock again, qilin and dragon appear together, sweet dew falls at night, choice grain springs up, and red grass and the like fill the court diary.

sentences.zh: 朕夙夜祗畏，上無以彰於先功，下無以克稱靈物。

translations.literal: Day and night I stand in awe; above I have no means to display the former merit, below no means worthily to match these numinous things.
translations.idiomatic: I tremble day and night: I cannot display the old achievements above, nor worthily answer these portents below.

sentences.zh: 漢遭秦餘，禮壞樂崩，且因循故事，未可觀省，有知其說者，各盡所能。

translations.literal: Han inherited the leavings of Qin; ritual was broken and music collapsed; moreover we have followed old precedents without being able to survey them—whoever knows the doctrine, let each exert his utmost.'
translations.idiomatic: Han followed Qin's wreck—rites in ruins, music lost—and we still coast on habit without a clear review. Whoever understands the system, give your best."

sentences.zh: 』褒省詔，乃嘆息謂諸生曰：『昔奚斯頌魯，考甫詠殷。

translations.literal: When Bao read the edict he sighed and said to the students: 'Formerly Xi Si praised Lu and Kaofu sang Yin.
translations.idiomatic: Reading this, Cao Bao sighed to his disciples: "Once Xi Si sang Lu's praise and Kaofu hymned Yin.

sentences.zh: 夫人臣依義顯君，竭忠彰主，行之美也。

translations.literal: For a minister to make clear his lord according to right, to exhaust loyalty and display the ruler, is the beauty of conduct.
translations.idiomatic: A minister's finest act is to illumine his ruler through duty and show his sovereign's worth through loyalty.

sentences.zh: 當仁不讓，吾何辭哉！

translations.literal: When it is time to be humane one does not yield—why should I decline!'
translations.idiomatic: When humanity calls, one does not step aside—why should I refuse?"

sentences.zh: 』遂復上疏，具陳禮樂之本，制改之意。

translations.literal: He therefore again submitted a memorial, setting forth in full the root of ritual and music and the intent of the reform.
translations.idiomatic: He sent another memorial laying out the foundations of ritual and music and what the reform should achieve.

sentences.zh: 拜褒侍中，從駕南巡，既還，以事下三公，未及奏，詔召玄武司馬班固，問改定禮制之宜。

translations.literal: Bao was appointed attendant-in-chief and accompanied the imperial carriage on the southern tour; after the return the matter was sent down to the three dukes, but before it could be memorialized an edict summoned Ban Gu, marshal of the Black Tortoise, to ask about the appropriateness of revising the ritual system.
translations.idiomatic: He was made attendant-in-chief and joined the southern tour; on return the case went to the three dukes, but before they reported the emperor called in Ban Gu of the Black Tortoise office to ask how ritual ought to be revised.

sentences.zh: 固曰：『京師諸儒，多能說禮，宜廣招集，共議得失。

translations.literal: Gu said: 'The scholars at the capital mostly can expound ritual; it is fitting broadly to summon and assemble them and jointly debate gains and losses.'
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu said: "Capital scholars mostly know ritual—call a wide conference and thrash out the pros and cons."

sentences.zh: 』帝曰：『諺言「作舍道邊，三年不成」。

translations.literal: The emperor said: 'The proverb runs, "Building a hut by the road, in three years it is not finished."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor answered: "The proverb says, Build a house beside the road and three years later it is still not done.

sentences.zh: 會禮之家，名為聚訟，互生疑異，筆不得下。

translations.literal: The houses that understand ritual are called gatherings of litigation; they breed doubt and contradiction and the brush cannot descend.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual specialists mean wrangling assemblies—suspicion on every side and no pen can move.

sentences.zh: 昔堯作《大章》，一夔足矣。』

translations.literal: Formerly Yao made the Da zhang—one Kui sufficed.'"
translations.idiomatic: When Yao composed the Da zhang, one Kui was enough."

sentences.zh: 章和元年正月，乃召褒詣嘉德門，令小黃門持班固所上叔孫通《漢儀》十二篇，敕褒曰：『此制散略，多不合經，今宜依禮條正，使可族行。

translations.literal: In the first month of the first year of the Zhanghe era he therefore summoned Bao to the Gate of Virtuous Blessing, had a minor yellow gate hold the twelve chapters of Shusun Tong's Han yi that Ban Gu had presented, and instructed Bao: 'This system is scattered and sketchy, much of it does not match the classics; now you should correct it according to ritual so it may be put into practice for all.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhanghe 1, first month, he called Cao Bao to the Gate of Virtuous Blessing, had a junior eunuch hand him the twelve-fascicle Han ceremonies of Shusun Tong that Ban Gu had submitted, and ordered: "This draft is loose and often uncanonical—revise it by the classics so it can be enforced everywhere.

sentences.zh: 於南宮、東觀盡心集作。

translations.literal: At the Southern Palace and the Eastern View devote your mind to compiling it.'
translations.idiomatic: Compile it with full care at the Southern Palace and the Eastern View."

sentences.zh: 』褒既受命，及次序禮事，依準舊典，雜以《五經》讖記之文，撰次天子至於庶人冠婚吉兇終始制度，以為百五十篇，寫以二尺四寸簡。

translations.literal: Bao, having received the mandate, thereupon ordered the ritual matters, relying on old canons and blending in the texts of the prognostications on the Five Classics, and arranged the institutions from capping and marriage through auspicious and inauspicious matters for the Son of Heaven down to the common people, making one hundred fifty chapters, written on slips two feet four inches long.
translations.idiomatic: Once commissioned he sequenced the rites from old precedents and Five-Classic weft-books, covering the Son of Heaven down to commoners—capping, weddings, lucky and unlucky rites from birth to death—in one hundred fifty chapters on two-foot-four slips.

sentences.zh: 其年十二月奏上。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month of that year he presented it.
translations.idiomatic: That December he presented the work.

sentences.zh: 帝以眾論難一，故但納之，不復令有司平奏。

translations.literal: Because the multitude of opinions could not be unified, the emperor therefore only accepted it and did not again order the officials to debate and memorialize.
translations.idiomatic: Opinions were too divided for the throne to impose one view, so the emperor filed the work away without ordering a full ministerial review.

sentences.zh: 會帝崩，和帝即位，褒乃為作章句，帝遂以《新禮》二篇冠。

translations.literal: When the emperor died and Emperor He ascended, Bao then composed commentaries on it; the emperor thereupon prefixed two chapters as the Xin li.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor died and He succeeded, Cao Bao wrote glosses; the new ruler then prefixed two fascicles as the New Rites.

sentences.zh: 擢褒監羽林左騎。

translations.literal: Bao was promoted to supervise the left wing of the feathered forest.
translations.idiomatic: He was raised to superintendent of the left wing of the feathered forest guard.

sentences.zh: 永元四年，遷射聲校尉。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of the Yongyuan era he was promoted to colonel of the shout-sheng corps.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 4 he became colonel of the shout-sheng corps.

sentences.zh: 後太尉張酺、尚書張敏等奏褒擅制《漢禮》，破亂聖術，宜加刑誅。

translations.literal: Later the grand commandant Zhang Fu, minister Zhang Min, and others memorialized that Bao had presumptuously compiled Han li, had disrupted the sages' art, and ought to suffer execution.
translations.idiomatic: Later Zhang Fu, Zhang Min, and others charged him with arrogating the Han ritual code and corrupting the sages' teaching—they demanded his death.

sentences.zh: 帝雖寢其奏，而《漢禮》遂不行。

translations.literal: Although the emperor shelved their memorial, Han li was never implemented.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor tabled the indictment, but Cao Bao's Han li never went into force.

sentences.zh: 褒在射聲，營舍有停棺不葬者百餘所，褒親自履行，問其意故。

translations.literal: While Bao was with the shout-sheng corps, in the camp quarters there were more than a hundred places where coffins lay unburied; Bao personally went about and asked the reasons.
translations.idiomatic: At the shout-sheng camp over a hundred coffins waited unburied; Cao Bao walked the lines and asked why.

sentences.zh: 吏對曰：『此等多是建武以來絕無後者，不得埋掩。

translations.literal: The clerks replied: 'These for the most part are persons without posterior heirs since the Jianwu era onward, and they could not obtain burial.'
translations.idiomatic: The officers said: "Most are people without heirs since Jianwu who could not afford burial."

sentences.zh: 』褒乃愴然，為買空地，悉葬其無主者，設祭以祀之。

translations.literal: Bao was deeply moved, bought open land, buried all those without masters, and set out offerings to sacrifice to them.
translations.idiomatic: Stricken, he bought vacant ground, interred every unclaimed body, and offered sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 遷城門校尉、將作大匠。

translations.literal: He was promoted to colonel of the city gates and superintendent of works.
translations.idiomatic: He moved up to colonel of the city gates and superintendent of works.

sentences.zh: 時有疾疫，褒巡行病徒，為致醫藥，經理饘粥，多蒙濟活。

translations.literal: When there was pestilence, Bao went among the sick, provided them physicians and medicine, and saw to gruel; many were saved by his care.
translations.idiomatic: During an epidemic he toured the sick wards, sent doctors and drugs, and saw that gruel was served—many lived because of him.

sentences.zh: 七年，出為河內太守。

translations.literal: In the seventh year he went out as grand administrator of Henei.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year he left the capital as prefect of Henei.

sentences.zh: 時春夏大旱，糧谷踴貴。

translations.literal: That spring and summer there was great drought and grain became dear.
translations.idiomatic: Spring and summer brought severe drought and soaring grain prices.

sentences.zh: 褒到，乃省吏並職，退去奸殘，澍雨數降。

translations.literal: When Bao arrived he reduced overlapping offices, dismissed wicked and cruel men, and timely rains fell several times.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival he merged redundant posts, purged cruel and corrupt officers, and soaking rains came again and again.

sentences.zh: 其秋大孰，百姓給足，流冗皆還。

translations.literal: That autumn there was a great harvest; the hundred surnames had enough, and wandering vagrants all returned.
translations.idiomatic: Autumn brought a bumper crop; households were fed and drifters came home.

sentences.zh: 後坐上災害不實免。

translations.literal: Later he was dismissed for sitting on a false report about disasters.
translations.idiomatic: He later lost office for misreporting calamities.

sentences.zh: 有頃征，再遷，復為侍中。

translations.literal: Before long he was summoned, was twice promoted, and again became attendant-in-chief.
translations.idiomatic: Soon recalled, he rose twice more and again became attendant-in-chief.

sentences.zh: 褒博物識古，為儒者宗。

translations.literal: Bao was broadly learned, knew antiquity, and was patriarch to the scholars.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Bao's learning spanned antiquity and made him dean to the Ru school.

sentences.zh: 十四年，卒官。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in the fourteenth year.

sentences.zh: 作《通義》十二篇，演經雜論百二十篇，又傳《禮記》四十九篇，教授諸生千餘人，慶氏學遂行於世。

translations.literal: He composed the Tong yi in twelve chapters, one hundred twenty chapters of expository essays on the classics, and in addition transmitted the forty-nine chapters of the Record of Rites; he instructed more than a thousand students, and the learning of the Qing lineage then spread in the world.
translations.idiomatic: He wrote twelve chapters of Comprehensive Meaning, one hundred twenty essays explicating the canon, transmitted the forty-nine-fascicle Book of Rites, taught over a thousand disciples, and established Qing's school in the world.

sentences.zh: 論曰：『漢初天下創定，朝制無文，叔孫通頗采經禮，參酌秦法，雖適物觀時，有救崩敝，然先王之容典蓋多闕矣，是以賈誼、仲舒、王吉、劉向之徒，懷憤嘆息所不能已也。

translations.literal: The judgment says: 'At the beginning of Han the realm was newly settled, court institutions had no literary form; Shusun Tong took somewhat from the canonical rites and consulted Qin law—though suited to things and observant of the times, and though it rescued collapse and decay, yet the outward forms of the former kings were for the most part missing; hence Jia Yi, Dong Zhongshu, Wang Ji, Liu Xiang, and their sort, harboring indignation and sighing, could not rest.
translations.idiomatic: The historian comments: "Early Han had no written court ritual; Shusun Tong drew on classical rites and Qin statutes—it met the moment and staunched decay, yet the old kings' forms were largely gone, which is why men like Jia Yi, Dong Zhongshu, Wang Ji, and Liu Xiang fumed and could not rest.

sentences.zh: 資文、宣之遠圖明懿，而終莫或用，故知自燕而觀，有不盡矣。

translations.literal: They had the distant plans and clear virtue of Emperors Wen and Xuan, yet in the end none of it was used—thus one knows that gazing from the swallow's nest, the view is not exhaustive.
translations.idiomatic: Wen and Xuan had long vision and clear virtue, yet none of these schemes was adopted—so viewing from a swallow's nest, one never sees the whole.

sentences.zh: 孝章永言前王，明發興作，專命禮臣，撰定國憲，洋洋乎盛德之事焉。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhang forever spoke of the former kings, dawned on the need to create, specially ordered the ritual ministers, and drafted the state's fundamental statutes—how vast a work of abundant virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangdi never ceased invoking the ancient kings, rose at dawn intent on creation, charged ritual officers, and drafted the national code—a magnificent act of virtue.

sentences.zh: 而業絕天算，議黜異端，斯道竟復墜矣。

translations.literal: Yet the enterprise was cut off by heaven's reckoning, debate dismissed divergent views, and this way in the end fell again.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven cut short his years, faction dismissed rival opinions, and the whole effort collapsed.

sentences.zh: 夫三王不相襲禮，五帝不相氵公樂，所以《咸》、《莖》異調，中都殊絕。

translations.literal: The Three Kings did not copy one another's ritual, the Five Emperors did not follow one another's music—therefore Xian and Jing had different modes and the central capitals were utterly unlike.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Kings did not inherit each other's rites nor the Five Emperors each other's music—hence the Xian and Jing odes differ and the two capitals stand worlds apart.

sentences.zh: 況物運遷回，情數萬化，制則不能隨其流變，品度未足定其滋章，斯固世主所當損益者也。

translations.literal: How much more when the movement of things turns and returns and the patterns of feeling shift in myriad ways—fixed regulations cannot follow every current change, and standards are not enough to settle their ever-growing complexity; this is surely what the rulers of the age ought to trim or enlarge.
translations.idiomatic: Far more today, as things revolve and feeling shifts endlessly—written rules cannot chase every change, nor can measures fix their swelling tangle; that is what each age's ruler must adjust.

sentences.zh: 且樂非夔、襄，而新音代起，律謝臯、蘇，而制令亟易，修補舊文，獨何猜焉？

translations.literal: Moreover music is not only Kui and Xiang, yet new tones arise in succession; pitch yields to Gao and Su, yet systems and commands change urgently—why be suspicious of mending the old texts alone?
translations.idiomatic: Music was never only Kui and Master Xiang, yet new airs keep coming; pitch was never only Gao and Su, yet scales and edicts change fast—why shrink from revising old texts?

sentences.zh: 禮云禮云，曷其然哉！

translations.literal: They speak of ritual, they speak of ritual—how can it be so!'
translations.idiomatic: They say "ritual, ritual"—can it really be only that?"

sentences.zh: 鄭玄字康成，北海高密人也。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan, courtesy name Kangcheng, was a native of Gaomi in Beihai.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan, style Kangcheng, came from Gaomi in Beihai commandery.

sentences.zh: 八世祖崇，哀帝時尚書仆射。

translations.literal: Eight generations back his ancestor Chong was vice-director of the secretariat in the time of Emperor Ai.
translations.idiomatic: Eight generations back, Zheng Chong had been vice-director of the secretariat under Emperor Ai.

sentences.zh: 玄少為鄉嗇夫，得休歸，嘗詣學官，不樂為吏，父數怒之，不能禁。

translations.literal: In his youth Xuan was village clerk for his township; when he had leave to return home he once visited the school official, disliked being a clerk, and his father repeatedly grew angry with him but could not stop him.
translations.idiomatic: As a youth he was a township clerk; on leave he visited the local school, found clerking distasteful, and though his father berated him repeatedly could not change his mind.

sentences.zh: 遂造太學受業，師事京兆第五元先，始通《京氏易》、《公羊春秋》、《三統歷》、《九章算術》。

translations.literal: He therefore went to the imperial academy to pursue studies, served as disciple to the fifth Yuanxian of the capital region, and first mastered the Jing lineage Changes, the Gongyang Spring and Autumn, the Triple Concordance calendar, and the Nine Chapters on mathematical procedures.
translations.idiomatic: He entered the imperial academy under Fifth Yuanxian of Jingzhao, mastering the Jing Changes, Gongyang Annals, Triple Concordance calendar, and Nine Chapters.

sentences.zh: 又從東郡張恭祖受《周官》、《禮記》、《左氏春秋》、《韓詩》、《古文尚書》。

translations.literal: He further received from Zhang Gongzu of Dong commandery the Rites of Zhou, the Record of Rites, the Zuo Spring and Autumn, the Han Odes, and the old-text Documents.
translations.idiomatic: From Zhang Gongzu of Dong he took the Rites of Zhou, Book of Rites, Zuo Annals, Han Odes, and old-text Documents.

sentences.zh: 以山東無兄問者，乃西入關，因涿郡盧植，事扶風馬融。

translations.literal: Because east of the mountains there was no one he could consult, he went west through the passes, and through Lu Zhi of Zhuo commandery served Ma Rong of Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Finding no teacher of stature east of the mountains, he crossed into the west and, introduced by Lu Zhi of Zhu, studied under Ma Rong of Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 融門徒四百餘人，升堂進者五十餘生。

translations.literal: Rong had more than four hundred disciples; those who ascended the hall numbered over fifty.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Rong had over four hundred disciples; more than fifty were admitted to the inner hall.

sentences.zh: 融素驕貴，玄在門下，三年不得見，乃使高業弟子傳授於玄。

translations.literal: Rong by nature was proud and lofty; Xuan was in his gate for three years without being granted an audience, and Rong therefore had advanced disciples transmit the teaching to Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Rong was haughty by nature; Zheng Xuan studied at his gate three years without an interview and learned only through Ma's senior pupils.

sentences.zh: 玄日夜尋誦，未嘗怠倦。

translations.literal: Xuan day and night pursued recitation and never slackened.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan read day and night without slackening.

sentences.zh: 會融集諸生考論圖緯，聞玄善算，乃召見於樓上，玄因從質諸疑義，問畢辭歸。

translations.literal: When Rong assembled the disciples to examine and debate the charts and weft-texts, he heard that Xuan was skilled in calculation and therefore summoned him to an audience in the upper story; Xuan thereupon questioned him on all doubtful points, and when the questions were finished he took leave and returned.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Rong gathered his students to debate charts and weft-books, he heard Zheng Xuan excelled at reckoning and summoned him upstairs; Zheng exhausted his doubts, then bowed out and left.

sentences.zh: 融喟然謂門人曰：『鄭生今去，吾道東矣。』

translations.literal: Rong sighed deeply and said to his disciples: 'When the Zheng student departs now, my way goes east.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Rong sighed to his disciples: "When Zheng goes east, my teaching travels with him."

sentences.zh: 玄自遊學，十餘年乃歸鄉裏。

translations.literal: From the time Xuan traveled for study, more than ten years passed before he returned to his home village.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten years passed in wandering study before he went home.

sentences.zh: 家貧，客耕東萊，學徒相隨已數百千人。

translations.literal: His family was poor; he hired out his plowing in Donglai, and students who followed him already numbered in the hundreds and thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Poor, he farmed for hire in Donglai while hundreds and then thousands of pupils trailed after him.

sentences.zh: 及黨事起，乃與同郡孫嵩等四十餘人俱被禁錮，遂隱修經業，杜門不出。

translations.literal: When the faction case arose, he together with more than forty men of his commandery such as Sun Song was all placed under prohibition, and he therefore retired to cultivate the classics behind closed doors and did not go out.
translations.idiomatic: When the partisan proscription struck, he and forty-odd countrymen including Sun Song were banned from office; he shut his doors, deepened his textual studies, and stayed in.

sentences.zh: 時任城何休好《公羊》學，遂著《公羊墨守》、《左氏膏肓》、《穀梁廢疾》；

translations.literal: At that time He Xiu of Rencheng loved Gongyang learning and therefore wrote Gongyang mo shou, Zuo shi gao huang, and Guliang fei ji;
translations.idiomatic: He Xiu of Rencheng, devoted to Gongyang, had written The Gongyang Fortress, The Zuo Vital Zone, and The Guliang Morbid Spots;

sentences.zh: 玄乃發《墨守》，針《膏肓》，起《廢疾》。

translations.literal: Xuan thereupon cracked Mo shou, needled Gao huang, and revived Fei ji.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan breached the Fortress, lanced the Vital Zone, and cured the Morbid Spots.

sentences.zh: 休見而嘆曰：『康成入吾室，操吾矛，以伐我乎！

translations.literal: When Xiu saw this he sighed and said: 'Kangcheng has entered my chamber, taken my spear, and come to attack me!'
translations.idiomatic: He Xiu exclaimed: "Kangcheng is in my room, wielding my own spear against me!

sentences.zh: 』初，中興之後，範升、陳元、李育、賈逵之徒爭論古今學，後馬融答北地太守劉瑰及玄答何休，義據通深，由是古學遂明。

translations.literal: At first, after the restoration, Fan Sheng, Chen Yuan, Li Yu, Jia Kui, and their sort debated old and new learning; later Ma Rong answered the grand administrator of Beidi Liu Gui and Xuan answered He Xiu—their arguments were thorough and deep, and thereby old-text learning became clear.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, after Guangwu, Fan Sheng, Chen Yuan, Li Yu, and Jia Kui had wrangled old versus new learning; later Ma Rong answered Liu Gui, prefect of Beidi, and Zheng Xuan answered He Xiu—with such depth that old-text scholarship carried the day.

sentences.zh: 靈帝末，黨禁解，大將軍何進聞而辟之。

translations.literal: At the end of the Ling Emperor's reign the partisan prohibition was lifted; the general-in-chief He Jin heard of him and summoned him.
translations.idiomatic: Near the end of Lingdi's reign the ban ended; He Jin the general-in-chief heard of him and called him in.

sentences.zh: 州郡以進權威，不敢違意，遂迫脅玄，不得已而詣之。

translations.literal: The commanderies and prefectures, because of Jin's awesome power, did not dare disobey his intent, and therefore coerced Xuan; unable to avoid it, he went to him.
translations.idiomatic: Local authorities, awed by He Jin, pressed Zheng Xuan until he had no choice but to present himself.

sentences.zh: 進為設幾杖，禮待甚優。

translations.literal: Jin for him set out a bench and staff and treated him with the utmost courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: He Jin set out a couch and cane and treated him with exceptional respect.

sentences.zh: 玄不受朝服，而以幅巾見。

translations.literal: Xuan did not accept court dress but met him wearing a plain headcloth.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan refused court robes and appeared in a scholar's cloth turban.

sentences.zh: 一宿逃去。

translations.literal: After one night he fled.
translations.idiomatic: He slipped away the next night.

sentences.zh: 時年六十，弟子河內趙商等自遠方至者數千。

translations.literal: At that time he was sixty; disciples such as Zhao Shang of Henei who came from distant places numbered in the thousands.
translations.idiomatic: He was sixty; disciples like Zhao Shang of Henei arrived from afar by the thousand.

sentences.zh: 後將軍袁隗表為侍中，以父喪不行。

translations.literal: Later General of the Rear Yuan Wei memorialized him as attendant-in-chief, but because of his father's mourning he did not take up the post.
translations.idiomatic: General of the Rear Yuan Wei recommended him for attendant-in-chief, but mourning for his father kept him from accepting.

sentences.zh: 國相孔融深敬於玄，履履造門。

translations.literal: The state chancellor Kong Rong deeply revered Xuan and, treading his slippers, went to his gate.
translations.idiomatic: State chancellor Kong Rong revered him deeply and hurried to his door in his house slippers.

sentences.zh: 告高密縣為玄特立一鄉，曰：『昔齊置「土鄉」，越有「君子軍」，皆異賢之意也。

translations.literal: He instructed Gaomi county to establish a separate township for Xuan, saying: 'Formerly Qi set up a "scholar township" and Yue had a "gentlemen's army"—all expressing esteem for the worthy.
translations.idiomatic: He told Gaomi county to carve out a special township, saying: "Qi once founded a 'scholar township' and Yue a 'gentlemen's host'—both honored worthies.

sentences.zh: 鄭君好學，實懷明德。

translations.literal: Lord Zheng loves learning and truly harbors bright virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Master Zheng loves learning and bears shining virtue.

sentences.zh: 昔太史公、廷尉吳公、謁者仆射鄧公，皆漢之名臣。

translations.literal: Formerly the grand scribe, minister of trials Wu, and chief herald Deng were all famous ministers of Han.
translations.idiomatic: The grand historian, Minister Wu of trials, and Chief Herald Deng were all celebrated Han ministers.

sentences.zh: 又南山四皓有園公、夏黃公，潛光隱耀，世嘉其高，皆悉稱公。

translations.literal: Moreover among the four hoary elders of South Mountain were Lord Yuan and Lord Xia Huang, who concealed their radiance; the age praised their loftiness, and all were styled lord.
translations.idiomatic: Among the South Mountain Four, Lord Yuan and Lord Xia Huang hid their light; the world praised their height, and each was called lord.

sentences.zh: 然則公者仁德之正號，不必三事大夫也。

translations.literal: Thus "lord" is the correct title for humane virtue; one need not be a minister of the three departments.
translations.idiomatic: So "lord" is the proper honor for humane excellence, not reserved for ministers of the three bureaus.

sentences.zh: 今鄭君鄉宜曰「鄭公鄉」。

translations.literal: Now Lord Zheng's township ought to be called "Lord Zheng township."
translations.idiomatic: Master Zheng's township should therefore be named the Township of Lord Zheng."

sentences.zh: 昔東海於公僅有一節，猶或戒鄉人侈其門閭，矧乃鄭公之德，而無駟牡之路！

translations.literal: Formerly Yu Gong of the Eastern Sea had only one good deed, yet he warned his fellow villagers to widen his gate; how much more Lord Zheng's virtue—and no road for a four-horse carriage!
translations.idiomatic: Even Yu Gong of Donghai, with a single good turn, told his neighbors to broaden his gate—how much more should we for Master Zheng's virtue, when his lane could not fit a team of four!

sentences.zh: 可廣開門衢，令容高車，號為「通德門」。』

translations.literal: You may widen the thoroughfare and gates to admit tall carts, and style it the Gate of Pervading Virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: Widen the lanes and gates for high-wheeled carriages and call the approach the Gate of Pervading Virtue."

sentences.zh: 董卓遷都長安，公卿舉玄為趙相，道斷不至。

translations.literal: When Dong Zhuo moved the capital to Chang'an, the dukes and ministers jointly recommended Xuan as chancellor of Zhao, but the roads were cut and he did not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo's move to Chang'an brought a nomination for Zheng Xuan as chancellor of Zhao, but blocked roads kept him from taking up the post.

sentences.zh: 會黃巾寇青部，乃避地徐州，徐州牧陶謙接以師友之禮。

translations.literal: When the Yellow Turbans raided the Qing region, he therefore removed to Xuzhou, and the shepherd of Xuzhou Tao Qian received him with the courtesy due a teacher and friend.
translations.idiomatic: When the Yellow Turbans ravaged Qing, he fled to Xuzhou, where prefect Tao Qian treated him as teacher and peer.

sentences.zh: 建安元年，自徐州還高密，道遇黃巾賊數萬人，見玄皆拜，相約不敢入縣境。

translations.literal: In the first year of the Jian'an era he returned from Xuzhou to Gaomi; on the road he encountered several tens of thousands of Yellow Turban bandits, who all bowed when they saw Xuan and agreed among themselves not to enter the county borders.
translations.idiomatic: In Jian'an 1, returning from Xuzhou to Gaomi, he met tens of thousands of Yellow Turbans who bowed to him and pledged not to cross into his county.

sentences.zh: 玄後嘗疾篤，自慮，以書戒子益恩曰：

translations.literal: Later Xuan once fell gravely ill, reflected on his situation, and with a letter admonished his son Yi'en, saying:
translations.idiomatic: When illness laid him low, he wrote to his son Yi'en:

sentences.zh: 吾家舊貧，不為父母群弟所容，去廝役之吏，遊學周、秦之都，往來幽、並、兗、豫之域，獲覲乎在位通人，處逸大儒，得意者咸從捧手，有所受焉。

translations.literal: Our family of old was poor and was not tolerated by parents or younger brothers; I left the clerk's drudgery and traveled to study in the capitals of Zhou and Qin, going back and forth in the regions of You, Bing, Yan, and Yu, was able to meet with eminent men in office and great scholars in reclusion, and those who were pleased all joined hands with me and I received something from each.
translations.idiomatic: We were once poor and unwelcome at home; I quit petty clerkship to study in the old Zhou and Qin capitals, wandered You, Bing, Yan, and Yu, met serving worthies and reclusive masters, and learned from all who would take my hand.

sentences.zh: 遂博稽《六藝》，粗覽傳記，時睹秘書緯術之奧。

translations.literal: Thereupon I broadly examined the Six Arts, roughly perused transmitted records, and at times glimpsed the subtleties of secret documents and weft arts.
translations.idiomatic: I mastered the Six Arts in breadth, skimmed historiography, and sometimes touched the arcana of palace weft-texts.

sentences.zh: 年過四十，乃歸供養，假田播殖，以娛朝夕。

translations.literal: Passing forty years of age, I returned to support my parents, borrowed fields to sow and reap, and passed the days in contentment.
translations.idiomatic: After forty I came home to care for my parents, rented land to farm, and lived out my days in quiet work.

sentences.zh: 遇閹尹擅勢，坐黨禁錮，十有四年，而蒙赦令，舉賢良方正有道，辟大將軍三司府。

translations.literal: I encountered eunuch ministers monopolizing power, sat in guilt for the faction case, and was proscribed for fourteen years; then I received an amnesty, was recommended as worthy, incorrupt, upright, and possessed of the Way, and was summoned to the general-in-chief's and three excellencies' offices.
translations.idiomatic: Then the eunuchs seized power; I fell under the partisan ban for fourteen years, was freed by amnesty, recommended worthy and upright, and summoned to the general-in-chief and the three departments.

sentences.zh: 公車再召，比牒並名，早為宰相。

translations.literal: The summons coach came twice; my name was paired on the registers with others who would soon have been chancellors.
translations.idiomatic: Twice the court coach called; my name was bundled on lists with men who would soon sit as chancellors.

sentences.zh: 惟彼數公，懿德大雅，克堪王臣，故宜式序。

translations.literal: Only those several gentlemen, of fine virtue and great refinement, could bear office as king's ministers; therefore they were fit to be ranked in order.
translations.idiomatic: Those men had the stature to serve a king and deserved their promotions.

sentences.zh: 吾自忖度，無任於此，但念述先聖之元意，思整百家之不齊，亦庶幾以竭吾才，故聞命罔從。

translations.literal: I measured myself and was not equal to that task; I only wished to set forth the original meaning of the former sages and bring order to the hundred schools' discord, and perhaps thereby exhaust my talent—therefore when I heard the command I did not obey.
translations.idiomatic: I knew I was not cut for high office; I wanted only to recover the sages' original intent and harmonize the warring schools—so I declined the summons.

sentences.zh: 而黃巾為害，萍浮南北，復歸邦鄉。

translations.literal: But the Yellow Turbans brought harm; I drifted duckweed north and south and returned to my homeland.
translations.idiomatic: Then the Turbans drove me to drift north and south until I could go home.

sentences.zh: 入此歲來，已七十矣。

translations.literal: Entering this year I am already seventy.
translations.idiomatic: This year I turn seventy.

sentences.zh: 宿素衰落，仍有失誤，案之禮典，便合傳家。

translations.literal: What I have long cultivated declines, and I still make mistakes; checked against the ritual canon, it is right to hand family affairs to the heir.
translations.idiomatic: My powers fail and I still err; by the rites it is time to pass the household on.

sentences.zh: 今我告爾以老，歸爾以事，將閑居以安性，賈思以終業。

translations.literal: Now I announce to you my old age and return affairs to you; I shall dwell at leisure to settle my nature and apply my mind to finish my life's work.
translations.idiomatic: I tell you I am old and leave the household in your hands; I will live quietly to steady my temper and bend my mind to complete my scholarly work.

sentences.zh: 自非拜國君之命，問族親之憂，展敬墳墓，觀省野物，胡嘗扶杖出門乎！

translations.literal: Unless it be to answer a ruler of the state's command, inquire into a clansman's trouble, pay respect at tombs, or observe things in the wilds—when have I ever leaned on a staff and gone out the gate!
translations.idiomatic: Save for state orders, kin in distress, graveside duty, or a look at the countryside, you will not see me leaning on a cane outside my gate.

sentences.zh: 家事大小，汝一承之。

translations.literal: Domestic matters great and small, you alone shall undertake them.
translations.idiomatic: You alone must manage everything at home, great or small.

sentences.zh: 咨爾焭々一夫，曾無同生相依。

translations.literal: Alas for you, solitary one—you have never had a full brother to rely on.
translations.idiomatic: You stand alone, my boy, with no brother to lean on.

sentences.zh: 其勖求君子之道，研鉆勿替，敬慎威儀，以近有德。

translations.literal: Strive for the way of the gentleman, study and bore without ceasing, be reverent and careful in bearing, and draw near to those who have virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Seek the gentleman's path, grind at your studies without slack, guard your deportment, and keep company with the virtuous.

sentences.zh: 顯譽成於僚友，德行立於已誌。

translations.literal: Manifest reputation is completed through colleagues; moral conduct is established through one's own resolve.
translations.idiomatic: A public name is forged among peers; character rests on your own will.

sentences.zh: 若致聲稱，亦有榮於所生，可不深念邪！

translations.literal: If you achieve reputation, there is also glory for those who bore you—should you not ponder deeply!
translations.idiomatic: Win honor and your parents share it—think long on that!

sentences.zh: 可不深念邪！

translations.literal: Can you fail to ponder this deeply!
translations.idiomatic: Let that thought sink deep!

sentences.zh: 吾雖無紱冕之緒，頗有讓爵之高。

translations.literal: Though I have no thread of cap-and-girdle rank, I have somewhat of the loftiness of declining noble titles.
translations.idiomatic: I never held high office, but I did refuse titles others craved.

sentences.zh: 自樂以論贊之功，庶不遺後人之羞，末所憤憤者，徒以亡親墳壟未成，所好群書率皆腐敝，不得於禮堂寫定，傳與其人。

translations.literal: I have been content to rely on the work of commentary and evaluation, hoping not to leave shame to later generations; what still vexes me is only that my late parents' tombs are not yet finished, that the many books I love are mostly decayed, and that I cannot copy them to completion in the ritual hall and transmit them to the right hands.
translations.idiomatic: I have been content with exegesis, hoping to spare posterity shame; what still gnaws is my parents' unfinished graves, my mold-eaten library, and my inability to finish fair copies in the lecture hall for worthy heirs.

sentences.zh: 日西方暮，其可圖乎！
