translations.literal: The sun is in the west and evening comes—can it still be planned for!
translations.idiomatic: The sun is low—what time is left to plan!

sentences.zh: 家今差多於昔，勤力務時，無恤饑寒。

translations.literal: The household is now somewhat better off than before; work hard at the seasons and do not fret over hunger and cold.
translations.idiomatic: We are a little better off than once; work the seasons hard and do not brood on want.

sentences.zh: 菲饑食，薄衣服，節夫二者，尚令吾寡恨。

translations.literal: Eat frugally when hungry, clothe thinly—moderate these two, and you will still leave me with few regrets.
translations.idiomatic: Plain food and spare clothing, if you hold to both, will leave me little to regret.

sentences.zh: 若忽忘不識，亦已焉哉！

translations.literal: If you heedlessly forget and do not understand, then so be it!'
translations.idiomatic: If you forget all this, then we are done talking."

sentences.zh: 時，大將軍袁紹總兵冀州，遣使要玄，大會賓客，玄最後至，乃延升上坐。

translations.literal: At that time the general-in-chief Yuan Shao commanded troops in Ji province, sent an envoy to invite Xuan, and held a great assembly of guests; Xuan arrived last and was then led up to the seat of honor.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shao of Ji invited him to a great banquet; Zheng Xuan came last and was shown to the highest seat.

sentences.zh: 身長八尺，飲酒一斛，秀眉明目，容儀溫偉。

translations.literal: He was eight feet tall, drank a hu of wine at a sitting, had elegant brows and bright eyes, and his bearing was mild and imposing.
translations.idiomatic: He stood eight feet, could drain a hu of wine at one sitting, had fine brows and clear eyes, and a warm, commanding presence.

sentences.zh: 紹客多豪俊，並有才說，見玄儒者，未以通人許之，競設異端，百家互起。

translations.literal: Shao's guests were mostly bold and outstanding, all possessed of talent and argument; seeing Xuan as a mere scholar, they did not grant him the status of a thorough man of the world, vied to pose strange positions, and the hundred schools rose against one another.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan's guests were swaggering debaters who, seeing a mere Ru, denied him mastery and tried every paradox from every school.

sentences.zh: 玄依方辯對，咸出問表，皆得所未聞，莫不嗟服。

translations.literal: Xuan answered each according to its proper category, all went beyond the questions, and all obtained what they had never heard; none did not sigh in admiration.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan met each challenge on its own terms, outpaced every question, and said what none had heard; they gasped in admiration.

sentences.zh: 時汝南應劭亦歸於紹，因自贊曰：『故太山太守應中遠，北面稱弟子何如？

translations.literal: At that time Ying Shao of Runan also had attached himself to Shao and therefore praised himself, saying: 'The former grand administrator of Taishan Ying Zhongyuan—how would it be to face north and call myself disciple?
translations.idiomatic: Ying Shao of Runan, also Yuan's client, boasted: "I am Ying Zhongyuan, late prefect of Taishan—may I face north as your disciple?

sentences.zh: 』玄笑曰：『仲尼之門考以四科，回、賜之徒不稱官閥。

translations.literal: 'Xuan smiled and said: 'At Confucius's gate the examination used four categories; disciples such as Hui and Ci did not proclaim official pedigrees.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan smiled: "Confucius tested disciples in four arts; men like Yan Hui and Duanmu Ci never traded on their offices."

sentences.zh: 』劭有慚色。

translations.literal: Shao had a shamefaced look.
translations.idiomatic: Ying Shao colored with embarrassment.

sentences.zh: 紹乃舉玄茂才，表為左中郎將，皆不就。

translations.literal: Shao thereupon recommended Xuan as flourishing talent and memorialized him as general of the household for the left, but in neither case did he accept.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shao then nominated him flourishing talent and recommended him as general of the household for the left; Zheng Xuan refused both.

sentences.zh: 公車征為大司農，給安車一乘，所過長吏送迎。

translations.literal: The summons coach summoned him as grand minister of agriculture, gave him one comfortable carriage, and the senior officials along the route saw him off and welcomed him.
translations.idiomatic: The court coach summoned him as grand minister of agriculture with a single cushioned carriage and escort from every magistrate along the way.

sentences.zh: 玄乃以病自乞還家。

translations.literal: Xuan thereupon pleaded illness on his own behalf and begged to return home.
translations.idiomatic: He pleaded illness and went home.

sentences.zh: 五年春，夢孔子告之曰：『起，起，今年歲在辰，來年歲在巳。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fifth year he dreamed that Confucius told him: 'Rise, rise; this year the year-star is in chen, next year it will be in si.'
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year's spring he dreamed Confucius said: "Up, up—the year-star stands in chen this year, in si the next."

sentences.zh: 』既寤，以讖合之，知命當終，有頃寢疾。

translations.literal: When he awoke he matched it to the prognostications, knew his fate was to end, and before long fell ill in bed.
translations.idiomatic: Awake, he read the weft-texts and knew his end was near; soon he took to his bed.

sentences.zh: 時袁紹與曹操相拒於官度，令其子譚遣使逼玄隨軍，不得已，載病到元城縣，疾篤不進，其年六月卒，年七十四。

translations.literal: At that time Yuan Shao and Cao Cao faced each other at Guandu; he ordered his son Tan to send an envoy to force Xuan to accompany the army; unable to avoid it, he carried his illness to Yuancheng county, grew too gravely ill to advance, and in the sixth month of that year died at age seventy-four.
translations.idiomatic: While Yuan Shao and Cao Cao faced off at Guandu, Yuan Tan sent men to drag him to camp; he yielded, rode sick to Yuancheng, could go no farther, and died there that sixth month at seventy-four.

sentences.zh: 遺令薄葬。

translations.literal: He left instructions for a plain burial.
translations.idiomatic: His testament called for a simple funeral.

sentences.zh: 自郡守以下嘗受業者，缞绖赴會千餘人。

translations.literal: From grand administrators downward, those who had once been his disciples, more than a thousand came in mourning cord to the assembly.
translations.idiomatic: Over a thousand former pupils, from prefects on down, came in hemp mourning.

sentences.zh: 門人相與撰玄答諸弟子問《五經》，依《論語》作《鄭誌》八篇。

translations.literal: The disciples together compiled Xuan's answers to disciples' questions on the Five Classics and, following the Analects, made the Zheng zhi in eight chapters.
translations.idiomatic: His students collected his replies on the Five Classics and, on the model of the Analects, produced the Zheng zhi in eight chapters.

sentences.zh: 凡玄所註《周易》、《尚書》、《毛詩》、《儀禮》、《禮記》、《論語》、《孝經》、《尚書大傳》、《中候》、《乾象歷》，又著《天文七政論》、《魯禮禘祫義》、《六藝論》、《毛詩譜》、《駁許慎五經異義》、《答臨孝存周禮難》，凡百餘萬言。

translations.literal: In all, Xuan's commentaries on the Zhou yi, Documents, Mao Odes, Ceremonial, Record of Rites, Analects, Classic of Filial Piety, Amplified Documents, Zhong hou, Qian xiang calendar, and moreover his compositions Astronomical treatise on the seven governors, Lu ritual meaning of di and xia, Treatise on the six arts, Mao ode genealogy, Refutation of Xu Shen's meanings of the five classics diverging, and Answer to Lin Xiaocun's objections to the Rites of Zhou—altogether over a million graphs.
translations.idiomatic: All told he glossed the Changes, Documents, Mao Odes, Ceremonial, Book of Rites, Analects, Filial Piety, Amplified Documents, Zhong hou, and the Qian xiang calendar, and wrote treatises on the seven celestial governors, Lu di and xia, the six arts, the Mao ode stemma, a refutation of Xu Shen's divergent glosses, and a reply to Lin Xiaocun's Zhou li critique—well over a million characters.

sentences.zh: 玄質於辭訓，通人頗譏其繁。

translations.literal: Xuan was plain in diction and gloss, and thorough men of the world rather ridiculed his verbosity.
translations.idiomatic: Critics found his glosses plain but wordy.

sentences.zh: 至於經傳洽孰，稱為純儒，齊、魯間宗之。

translations.literal: As for his thorough mastery of the classics and commentaries, he was called a pure scholar, and between Qi and Lu they took him as their authority.
translations.idiomatic: On mastery of canon and commentary he was judged a pure Ru, and Qi and Lu bowed to him.

sentences.zh: 其門人山陽郗慮至禦史大夫，東萊王基、清河崔琰著名於世。

translations.literal: Among his disciples Xi Lu of Shanyang rose to imperial counsellor, Wang Ji of Donglai and Cui Yan of Qinghe became famous in the world.
translations.idiomatic: His pupils included Xi Lu of Shanyang, who reached imperial counsellor, and Wang Ji of Donglai and Cui Yan of Qinghe, who became famous.

sentences.zh: 又樂安國淵、任嘏，時並童幼，玄稱淵為國器，嘏有道德，其餘亦多所鑒拔，皆如其言。

translations.literal: Moreover Guo Yuan of Le'an and Ren Gu were at that time still in childhood; Xuan called Yuan a vessel of the state and Gu possessed of virtue; as for the rest he also appraised and promoted many, and all turned out as he said.
translations.idiomatic: As boys, Guo Yuan of Le'an and Ren Gu drew his praise—Yuan a state vessel, Gu a moral nature—and his other judgments likewise proved true.

sentences.zh: 玄唯有一子益恩，孔融在北海，舉為孝廉；

translations.literal: Xuan had only one son, Yi'en; when Kong Rong was in Beihai he recommended him as filial and incorrupt;
translations.idiomatic: His only son Yi'en was recommended filial and honest while Kong Rong governed Beihai;

sentences.zh: 及融為黃巾所圍，益恩赴難損身。

translations.literal: when Rong was besieged by the Yellow Turbans, Yi'en went to his rescue and lost his life.
translations.idiomatic: when Kong Rong was trapped by the Turbans, Yi'en rushed to help and was killed.

sentences.zh: 有遺腹子，玄以其手文似己，名之曰小同。

translations.literal: There was a posthumous child; Xuan because the lines on his hand resembled his own named him Xiaotong.
translations.idiomatic: A posthumous child was born; Zheng Xuan, seeing palm lines like his own, named the boy Xiaotong.

sentences.zh: 論曰：自秦焚《六經》，聖文埃滅。

translations.literal: The judgment says: From the time Qin burned the Six Arts, the sage texts were dust and extinction.
translations.idiomatic: The historian writes: Qin's burning of the Six Classics left the sages' words in ash.

sentences.zh: 漢興，諸儒頗修藝文；

translations.literal: When Han arose, the various scholars somewhat restored the arts and documents;
translations.idiomatic: Han brought scholars who began to restore the canon;

sentences.zh: 及東京，學者亦各名家。

translations.literal: by Eastern Capital times, students too each became masters in their own names.
translations.idiomatic: by the Eastern Han each school claimed its own master.

sentences.zh: 而守文之徒，滯固所稟，異端紛紜，互相詭激，遂令經有數家，家有數說，章句多者或乃百餘萬言，學徒勞而少功，後生疑而莫正。

translations.literal: Yet those who clung to the letter, stuck in what they had received, with divergent doctrines tangled and mutually provoking, thereupon let the classics have several houses and each house several explanations, and in some cases glosses ran to over a million graphs—disciples toiled with little gain, and the young doubted and had no standard.
translations.idiomatic: Literalists clung to sect, schools crossed swords, every classic spawned rival houses and every house rival glosses—some running to a million characters—so students labored without profit and youth had no touchstone.

sentences.zh: 鄭玄括囊大典，網羅眾家，刪裁繁誣，刊改漏失，自是學者略知所歸。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan gathered the great canons into a bag, netted the many houses, pruned the excessive and false, corrected omissions and errors, and from this scholars had a rough idea where to turn.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan gathered the great texts, sifted every school, cut bombast and error, and gave scholars a compass.

sentences.zh: 王父豫章君每考先儒經訓，而長於玄，常以為仲尼之門不能過也。

translations.literal: My paternal grandfather the Lord of Yuzhang whenever he examined the glosses of former scholars on the classics always ranked Xuan above them and constantly held that not even Confucius's gate could surpass him.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Ye's grandfather, the lord of Yuzhang, collated earlier masters' glosses and always put Zheng Xuan first, convinced that even Confucius's school did not outrank him.

sentences.zh: 及傳授生徒，並專以鄭氏家法云。

translations.literal: When in turn he instructed students, he used exclusively the Zheng family method.'
translations.idiomatic: When he taught pupils himself, he followed only the Zheng method."

sentences.zh: 贊曰：富平之緒，承家載世。

translations.literal: The encomium says: The thread of Fuping, inheriting the house through generations.
translations.idiomatic: The verse says: Fuping's line, handed down age after age.

sentences.zh: 伯仁先歸，厘我國祭。

translations.literal: Boren came home first and set our state sacrifices in order.
translations.idiomatic: Boren returned first and straightened the dynastic cult.

sentences.zh: 玄定義乖，褒修禮缺。

translations.literal: Xuan fixed meanings where they had gone astray; Bao repaired what ritual lacked.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan set wandering glosses right; Cao Bao patched missing rite.

sentences.zh: 孔書遂明，漢章中輟。

translations.literal: The writings of Kong grew clear; Han's statutes halted midway.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius's canon grew clear; Han's code stalled halfway.
